hints.to come: That would be 'venire'; 'supervenire' means 'to come upon'

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'supervenit'; 'superveniet' with -iet is future
hints.perfect: Perfect would be 'supervenit' (same spelling, different quantity)

hints.venio, venire, veni, ventum: These are the principal parts of 'venio' (to come); 'supervenio' is a compound
hints.invenio, invenire, inveni, inventum: These are the principal parts of 'invenio' (to find)
hints.advenio, advenire, adveni, adventum: These are the principal parts of 'advenio' (to arrive)

hints.Sanctus: 'Sanctus' is an adjective modifying 'Spiritus', not the subject by itself
hints.superveniet: 'Superveniet' is the verb, not the subject

hints.genitive: Genitive singular would also be 'spiritus' (4th decl.), but here it is the subject

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension -us nouns have genitive -i; 'spiritus' has genitive 'spiritus' (-us)
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has -is in the genitive; 'spiritus' has genitive 'spiritus'
hints.5th declension: 5th declension nouns end in -es; 'spiritus' is a 4th declension -us noun

hints.ablative: Ablative would also be 'te', but after 'in' + acc. it indicates motion toward

translation: The Holy Spirit will come upon you
